qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Go binary-protocol container size overflow enables preallocation-driven server OOM
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fix adds a recursion-depth limit to Node.js protocol `skip()` handlers, while this finding is a Go-specific integer overflow in `ReadListBegin` that leads to oversized slice preallocation and out-of-memory termination.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Node.js uncontrolled-recursion root cause.
evidence: The advisory is `GHSA-r67j-r569-jrwp` / `CVE-2026-41636`, summarized as `Apache Thrift Node.js bindings vulnerable to Uncontrolled Recursion`.

Apache Thrift commit `4af8c7c8768cb687b182c1839a39d4beb4f35a5d` between `v0.22.0` and `v0.23.0` fixes the issue by adding a depth parameter and 64-level limit to Node.js `skip()` in `lib/nodejs/lib/thrift/binary_protocol.js`, `compact_protocol.js`, and `json_protocol.js`.

The only real qualifying finding in the session manifest is `go_container_size_overflow_dos`, which cites `lib/go/thrift/binary_protocol.go:385`, `lib/go/thrift/configuration.go:319`, and generated Go deserializers as the vulnerable code path.
That finding's exploit path is a Go binary-protocol container-size integer overflow leading to slice preallocation OOM, not uncontrolled recursion in the Node.js `skip()` implementation.
